name: gotrue

metadata.name: gotrue
metadata.summary: Python Client Library for Supabase Auth

documentation:
# Auth-py

[![License: MIT](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-MIT-green.svg?label=license)](https://opensource.org/licenses/MIT)
[![CI](https://github.com/supabase-community/gotrue-py/actions/workflows/ci.yml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/supabase-community/gotrue-py/actions/workflows/ci.yml)
[![Python](https://img.shields.io/pypi/pyversions/gotrue)](https://pypi.org/project/gotrue)
[![Version](https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/gotrue?color=%2334D058)](https://pypi.org/project/gotrue)
[![Codecov](https://codecov.io/gh/supabase-community/gotrue-py/branch/main/graph/badge.svg)](https://codecov.io/gh/supabase-community/gotrue-py)
[![Last commit](https://img.shields.io/github/last-commit/supabase-community/gotrue-py.svg?style=flat)](https://github.com/supabase-community/gotrue-py/commits)
[![GitHub commit activity](https://img.shields.io/github/commit-activity/m/supabase-community/gotrue-py)](https://github.com/supabase-community/gotrue-py/commits)
[![Github Stars](https://img.shields.io/github/stars/supabase-community/gotrue-py?style=flat&logo=github)](https://github.com/supabase-community/gotrue-py/stargazers)
[![Github Forks](https://img.shields.io/github/forks/supabase-community/gotrue-py?style=flat&logo=github)](https://github.com/supabase-community/gotrue-py/network/members)
[![Github Watchers](https://img.shields.io/github/watchers/supabase-community/gotrue-py?style=flat&logo=github)](https://github.com/supabase-community/gotrue-py)
[![GitHub contributors](https://img.shields.io/github/contributors/supabase-community/gotrue-py)](https://github.com/supabase-community/gotrue-py/graphs/contributors)

This is a Python port of the [supabase js gotrue client](https://github.com/supabase/gotrue-js). The current state is that there is a features parity but with small differences that are mentioned in the section **Differences to the JS client**. As of December 14th, we renamed to repo from `gotrue-py` to `auth-py` to mirror the changes in the JavaScript library.

## Installation

We are still working on making the `gotrue` python library more user-friendly. For now here are some sparse notes on how to install the module.

### Poetry

```bash
poetry add gotrue
```

### Pip

```bash
pip install gotrue
```

## Differences to the JS client

It should be noted there are differences to the [JS client](https://github.com/supabase/gotrue-js). If you feel particulaly strongly about them and want to motivate a change, feel free to make a GitHub issue and we can discuss it there.

Firstly, feature pairity is not 100% with the [JS client](https://github.com/supabase/gotrue-js). In most cases we match the methods and attributes of the [JS client](https://github.com/supabase/gotrue-js) and api classes, but is some places (e.g for browser specific code) it didn't make sense to port the code line for line.

There is also a divergence in terms of how errors are raised. In the [JS client](https://github.com/supabase/gotrue-js), the errors are returned as part of the object, which the user can choose to process in whatever way they see fit. In this Python client, we raise the errors directly where they originate, as it was felt this was more Pythonic and adhered to the idioms of the language more directly.

In JS we return the error, but in Python we just raise it.

```js
const { data, error } = client.sign_up(...)
```

The other key difference is we do not use pascalCase to encode variable and method names. Instead we use the snake_case convention adopted in the Python language.

Also, the `gotrue` library for Python parses the date-time string into `datetime` Python objects. The [JS client](https://github.com/supabase/gotrue-js) keeps the date-time as strings.

## Usage (outdated)

**Important:** This section is outdated, you can be guided by the [JS client documentation](https://supabase.github.io/gotrue-js) because this Python client has a lot of parity with the JS client.

To instantiate the client, you'll need the URL and any request headers at a minimum.

```python
from gotrue import SyncGoTrueClient

headers = {
    "apiKey": "my-mega-awesome-api-key",
    # ... any other headers you might need.
}
client: SyncGoTrueClient = SyncGoTrueClient(url="www.genericauthwebsite.com", headers=headers)
```

To send a magic email link to the user, just provide the email kwarg to the `sign_in` method:

```python
user: Dict[str, Any] = client.sign_up(email="example@gmail.com")
```

To login with email and password, provide both to the `sign_in` method:

```python
user: Dict[str, Any] = client.sign_up(email="example@gmail.com", password="*********")
```

To sign out of the logged in user, call the `sign_out` method. We can then assert that the session and user are null values.

```python
client.sign_out()
assert client.user() is None
assert client.session() is None
```

We can refesh a users session.

```python
# The user should already be signed in at this stage.
user = client.refresh_session()
assert client.user() is not None
assert client.session() is not None
```

## Contributions

We would be immensely grateful for any contributions to this project.

classes.name: gotrue._sync.gotrue_mfa_api.SyncGoTrueMFAAPI

methods.name: gotrue._sync.gotrue_mfa_api.SyncGoTrueMFAAPI.enroll
documentation:
Starts the enrollment process for a new Multi-Factor Authentication
factor. This method creates a new factor in the 'unverified' state.
Present the QR code or secret to the user and ask them to add it to their
authenticator app. Ask the user to provide you with an authenticator code
from their app and verify it by calling challenge and then verify.

The first successful verification of an unverified factor activates the
factor. All other sessions are logged out and the current one gets an
`aal2` authenticator level.

methods.name: gotrue._sync.gotrue_mfa_api.SyncGoTrueMFAAPI.challenge
documentation:
Prepares a challenge used to verify that a user has access to a MFA
factor. Provide the challenge ID and verification code by calling `verify`.

methods.name: gotrue._sync.gotrue_mfa_api.SyncGoTrueMFAAPI.challenge_and_verify
documentation:
Helper method which creates a challenge and immediately uses the given code
to verify against it thereafter. The verification code is provided by the
user by entering a code seen in their authenticator app.

methods.name: gotrue._sync.gotrue_mfa_api.SyncGoTrueMFAAPI.verify
documentation:
Verifies a verification code against a challenge. The verification code is
provided by the user by entering a code seen in their authenticator app.

methods.name: gotrue._sync.gotrue_mfa_api.SyncGoTrueMFAAPI.unenroll
documentation:
Unenroll removes a MFA factor. Unverified factors can safely be ignored
and it's not necessary to unenroll them. Unenrolling a verified MFA factor
cannot be done from a session with an `aal1` authenticator level.

methods.name: gotrue._sync.gotrue_mfa_api.SyncGoTrueMFAAPI.list_factors
documentation:
Returns the list of MFA factors enabled for this user. For most use cases
you should consider using `get_authenticator_assurance_level`.

This uses a cached version of the factors and avoids incurring a network call.
If you need to update this list, call `get_user` first.

methods.name: gotrue._sync.gotrue_mfa_api.SyncGoTrueMFAAPI.get_authenticator_assurance_level
documentation:
Returns the Authenticator Assurance Level (AAL) for the active session.

- `aal1` (or `null`) means that the user's identity has been verified only
with a conventional login (email+password, OTP, magic link, social login,
etc.).
- `aal2` means that the user's identity has been verified both with a
conventional login and at least one MFA factor.

Although this method returns a promise, it's fairly quick (microseconds)
and rarely uses the network. You can use this to check whether the current
user needs to be shown a screen to verify their MFA factors.
Contains the full multi-factor authentication API.

documentation: Lists all factors attached to a user.

methods.name: gotrue._sync.gotrue_admin_mfa_api.SyncGoTrueAdminMFAAPI.delete_factor
documentation:
Deletes a factor on a user. This will log the user out of all active
sessions (if the deleted factor was verified). There's no need to delete
unverified factors.
Contains the full multi-factor authentication administration API.

methods.name: gotrue._sync.client.SyncGoTrueClient.__init__
documentation:
Create a new client

url : str
    The URL of the GoTrue server.
headers : Dict[str, str]
    Any additional headers to send to the GoTrue server.
auto_refresh_token : bool
    Set to "true" if you want to automatically refresh the token before
    expiring.
persist_session : bool
    Set to "true" if you want to automatically save the user session
    into local storage.
local_storage : SupportedStorage
    The storage engine to use for persisting the session.
cookie_options : CookieOptions
    The options for the cookie.
verify: bool
    Verify SSL, True by default, False disables verification.
proxy: str
    HTTP Proxy string or None, None by default, None disables proxy.

methods.name: gotrue._sync.client.SyncGoTrueClient.init_recover
documentation: Recover the current session from local storage.

methods.name: gotrue._sync.client.SyncGoTrueClient.sign_up
documentation:
Creates a new user. If email and phone are provided, email will be
used and phone will be ignored.

Parameters
---------
email : Optional[str]
    The user's email address.
phone : Optional[str]
    The user's phone number.
password : Optional[str]
    The user's password.
redirect_to : Optional[str]
    A URL or mobile address to send the user to after they are confirmed.
data : Optional[Dict[str, Any]]
    Optional user metadata.

Returns
-------
response : Union[Session, User]
    A logged-in session if the server has "autoconfirm" ON
    A user if the server has "autoconfirm" OFF

Raises
------
APIError
    If an error occurs.

methods.name: gotrue._sync.client.SyncGoTrueClient.sign_in
documentation:
Log in an existing user, or login via a third-party provider.
If email and phone are provided, email will be used and phone will be ignored.

Parameters
---------
email : Optional[str]
    The user's email address.
phone : Optional[str]
    The user's phone number.
password : Optional[str]
    The user's password.
refresh_token : Optional[str]
    A valid refresh token that was returned on login.
provider : Optional[Provider]
    One of the providers supported by GoTrue.
redirect_to : Optional[str]
    A URL or mobile address to send the user to after they are confirmed.
scopes : Optional[str]
    A space-separated list of scopes granted to the OAuth application.

Returns
-------
response : Optional[Union[Session, str]]
    If only email are provided between the email and password,
    None is returned and send magic link to email

    If email and password are provided, a logged-in session is returned.

    If only phone are provided between the phone and password,
    None is returned and send message to phone

    If phone and password are provided, a logged-in session is returned.

    If refresh_token is provided, a logged-in session is returned.

    If provider is provided, an redirect URL is returned.

    Otherwise, error is raised.

Raises
------
APIError
    If an error occurs.

methods.name: gotrue._sync.client.SyncGoTrueClient.verify_otp

Parameters

    If an error occurs.

APIError

classes.name: gotrue._async.gotrue_mfa_api.AsyncGoTrueMFAAPI

methods.name: gotrue._async.gotrue_mfa_api.AsyncGoTrueMFAAPI.enroll
documentation:
Starts the enrollment process for a new Multi-Factor Authentication
factor. This method creates a new factor in the 'unverified' state.
Present the QR code or secret to the user and ask them to add it to their
authenticator app. Ask the user to provide you with an authenticator code
from their app and verify it by calling challenge and then verify.

The first successful verification of an unverified factor activates the
factor. All other sessions are logged out and the current one gets an
`aal2` authenticator level.

methods.name: gotrue._async.gotrue_mfa_api.AsyncGoTrueMFAAPI.challenge
documentation:
Prepares a challenge used to verify that a user has access to a MFA
factor. Provide the challenge ID and verification code by calling `verify`.

methods.name: gotrue._async.gotrue_mfa_api.AsyncGoTrueMFAAPI.challenge_and_verify
documentation:
Helper method which creates a challenge and immediately uses the given code
to verify against it thereafter. The verification code is provided by the
user by entering a code seen in their authenticator app.

methods.name: gotrue._async.gotrue_mfa_api.AsyncGoTrueMFAAPI.verify
documentation:
Verifies a verification code against a challenge. The verification code is
provided by the user by entering a code seen in their authenticator app.

methods.name: gotrue._async.gotrue_mfa_api.AsyncGoTrueMFAAPI.unenroll
documentation:
Unenroll removes a MFA factor. Unverified factors can safely be ignored
and it's not necessary to unenroll them. Unenrolling a verified MFA factor
cannot be done from a session with an `aal1` authenticator level.

methods.name: gotrue._async.gotrue_mfa_api.AsyncGoTrueMFAAPI.list_factors
documentation:
Returns the list of MFA factors enabled for this user. For most use cases
you should consider using `get_authenticator_assurance_level`.

This uses a cached version of the factors and avoids incurring a network call.
If you need to update this list, call `get_user` first.

methods.name: gotrue._async.gotrue_mfa_api.AsyncGoTrueMFAAPI.get_authenticator_assurance_level
documentation:
Returns the Authenticator Assurance Level (AAL) for the active session.

- `aal1` (or `null`) means that the user's identity has been verified only
with a conventional login (email+password, OTP, magic link, social login,
etc.).
- `aal2` means that the user's identity has been verified both with a
conventional login and at least one MFA factor.

Although this method returns a promise, it's fairly quick (microseconds)
and rarely uses the network. You can use this to check whether the current
user needs to be shown a screen to verify their MFA factors.
Contains the full multi-factor authentication API.

methods.name: gotrue._async.gotrue_admin_api.AsyncGoTrueAdminAPI.sign_out
documentation: Removes a logged-in session.

methods.name: gotrue._async.gotrue_admin_api.AsyncGoTrueAdminAPI.invite_user_by_email
documentation: Sends an invite link to an email address.

methods.name: gotrue._async.gotrue_admin_api.AsyncGoTrueAdminAPI.generate_link
documentation: Generates email links and OTPs to be sent via a custom email provider.

methods.name: gotrue._async.gotrue_admin_api.AsyncGoTrueAdminAPI.create_user
documentation:
Creates a new user.

This function should only be called on a server.
Never expose your `service_role` key in the browser.
User Admin API

methods.name: gotrue._async.gotrue_admin_api.AsyncGoTrueAdminAPI.list_users
documentation:
Get a list of users.

This function should only be called on a server.
Never expose your `service_role` key in the browser.

methods.name: gotrue._async.gotrue_admin_api.AsyncGoTrueAdminAPI.get_user_by_id
documentation:
Get user by id.

This function should only be called on a server.
Never expose your `service_role` key in the browser.

methods.name: gotrue._async.gotrue_admin_api.AsyncGoTrueAdminAPI.update_user_by_id
documentation:
Updates the user data.

This function should only be called on a server.
Never expose your `service_role` key in the browser.

methods.name: gotrue._async.gotrue_admin_api.AsyncGoTrueAdminAPI.delete_user
documentation:
Delete a user. Requires a `service_role` key.

This function should only be called on a server.
Never expose your `service_role` key in the browser.

methods.name: gotrue._sync.gotrue_client.SyncGoTrueClient.initialize
documentation: Initializations

methods.name: gotrue._sync.gotrue_client.SyncGoTrueClient.sign_in_anonymously
documentation: Creates a new anonymous user.
Public methods

methods.name: gotrue._sync.gotrue_client.SyncGoTrueClient.sign_up
documentation: Creates a new user.

methods.name: gotrue._sync.gotrue_client.SyncGoTrueClient.sign_in_with_password
documentation: Log in an existing user with an email or phone and password.

methods.name: gotrue._sync.gotrue_client.SyncGoTrueClient.sign_in_with_id_token
documentation: Allows signing in with an OIDC ID token. The authentication provider used should be enabled and configured.

methods.name: gotrue._sync.gotrue_client.SyncGoTrueClient.sign_in_with_sso
documentation:
Attempts a single-sign on using an enterprise Identity Provider. A
successful SSO attempt will redirect the current page to the identity
provider authorization page. The redirect URL is implementation and SSO
protocol specific.

You can use it by providing a SSO domain. Typically you can extract this
domain by asking users for their email address. If this domain is
registered on the Auth instance the redirect will use that organization's
currently active SSO Identity Provider for the login.
If you have built an organization-specific login page, you can use the
organization's SSO Identity Provider UUID directly instead.

methods.name: gotrue._sync.gotrue_client.SyncGoTrueClient.sign_in_with_oauth
documentation: Log in an existing user via a third-party provider.

methods.name: gotrue._sync.gotrue_client.SyncGoTrueClient.sign_in_with_otp
documentation:
Log in a user using magiclink or a one-time password (OTP).

If the `{{ .ConfirmationURL }}` variable is specified in
the email template, a magiclink will be sent.

If the `{{ .Token }}` variable is specified in the email
template, an OTP will be sent.

If you're using phone sign-ins, only an OTP will be sent.
You won't be able to send a magiclink for phone sign-ins.

methods.name: gotrue._sync.gotrue_client.SyncGoTrueClient.resend
documentation: Resends an existing signup confirmation email, email change email, SMS OTP or phone change OTP.

methods.name: gotrue._sync.gotrue_client.SyncGoTrueClient.verify_otp
documentation: Log in a user given a User supplied OTP received via mobile.

methods.name: gotrue._sync.gotrue_client.SyncGoTrueClient.get_session
documentation:
Returns the session, refreshing it if necessary.

The session returned can be null if the session is not detected which
can happen in the event a user is not signed-in or has logged out.

methods.name: gotrue._sync.gotrue_client.SyncGoTrueClient.get_user
documentation:
Gets the current user details if there is an existing session.

Takes in an optional access token `jwt`. If no `jwt` is provided,
`get_user()` will attempt to get the `jwt` from the current session.

methods.name: gotrue._sync.gotrue_client.SyncGoTrueClient.update_user
documentation: Updates user data, if there is a logged in user.

methods.name: gotrue._sync.gotrue_client.SyncGoTrueClient.set_session
documentation:
Sets the session data from the current session. If the current session
is expired, `set_session` will take care of refreshing it to obtain a
new session.

If the refresh token in the current session is invalid and the current
session has expired, an error will be thrown.

If the current session does not contain at `expires_at` field,
`set_session` will use the exp claim defined in the access token.

The current session that minimally contains an access token,
refresh token and a user.

methods.name: gotrue._sync.gotrue_client.SyncGoTrueClient.refresh_session
documentation:
Returns a new session, regardless of expiry status.

Takes in an optional current session. If not passed in, then refreshSession()
will attempt to retrieve it from getSession(). If the current session's
refresh token is invalid, an error will be thrown.

methods.name: gotrue._sync.gotrue_client.SyncGoTrueClient.sign_out
documentation:
Inside a browser context, `sign_out` will remove the logged in user from the
browser session and log them out - removing all items from localstorage and
then trigger a `"SIGNED_OUT"` event.

For server-side management, you can revoke all refresh tokens for a user by
passing a user's JWT through to `api.sign_out`.

There is no way to revoke a user's access token jwt until it expires.
It is recommended to set a shorter expiry on the jwt for this reason.

methods.name: gotrue._sync.gotrue_client.SyncGoTrueClient.on_auth_state_change
documentation: Receive a notification every time an auth event happens.

methods.name: gotrue._sync.gotrue_client.SyncGoTrueClient.reset_password_for_email
documentation: Sends a password reset request to an email address.

methods.name: gotrue._sync.gotrue_client.SyncGoTrueClient.reset_password_email
documentation: Sends a password reset request to an email address.

methods.name: gotrue._sync.gotrue_client.SyncGoTrueClient._enroll
documentation: MFA methods

methods.name: gotrue._sync.gotrue_client.SyncGoTrueClient._remove_session
documentation: Private methods

methods.name: gotrue._sync.gotrue_client.SyncGoTrueClient._decode_jwt
documentation: Decodes a JWT (without performing any validation).

classes.name: gotrue.types.AMREntry

class_variables.name: gotrue.types.AMREntry.method
documentation: Authentication method name.

class_variables.name: gotrue.types.AMREntry.timestamp
documentation:
Timestamp when the method was successfully used. Represents number of
seconds since 1st January 1970 (UNIX epoch) in UTC.

An authentication methord reference (AMR) entry.

An entry designates what method was used by the user to verify their
identity and at what time.

class_variables.name: gotrue.types.Session.provider_token
documentation:
The oauth provider token. If present, this can be used to make external API
requests to the oauth provider used.

class_variables.name: gotrue.types.Session.provider_refresh_token
documentation:
The oauth provider refresh token. If present, this can be used to refresh
the provider_token via the oauth provider's API.

Not all oauth providers return a provider refresh token. If the
provider_refresh_token is missing, please refer to the oauth provider's
documentation for information on how to obtain the provider refresh token.

class_variables.name: gotrue.types.Session.expires_in
documentation:
The number of seconds until the token expires (since it was issued).
Returned when a login is confirmed.

class_variables.name: gotrue.types.Session.expires_at
documentation: A timestamp of when the token will expire. Returned when a login is confirmed.

classes.name: gotrue.types.Factor

class_variables.name: gotrue.types.Factor.id
documentation: ID of the factor.

class_variables.name: gotrue.types.Factor.friendly_name
documentation: Friendly name of the factor, useful to disambiguate between multiple factors.

class_variables.name: gotrue.types.Factor.factor_type
documentation:
Type of factor. Only `totp` supported with this version but may change in
future versions.

class_variables.name: gotrue.types.Factor.status
documentation: Factor's status.
A MFA factor.

class_variables.name: gotrue.types.AdminUserAttributes.role
documentation:
The `role` claim set in the user's access token JWT.

When a user signs up, this role is set to `authenticated` by default. You should only modify the `role` if you need to provision several levels of admin access that have different permissions on individual columns in your database.

Setting this role to `service_role` is not recommended as it grants the user admin privileges.

class_variables.name: gotrue.types.AdminUserAttributes.password_hash
documentation:
The `password_hash` for the user's password.

Allows you to specify a password hash for the user. This is useful for migrating a user's password hash from another service.

Supports bcrypt and argon2 password hashes.

class_variables.name: gotrue.types.AdminUserAttributes.id
documentation:
The `id` for the user.

Allows you to overwrite the default `id` set for the user.

class_variables.name: gotrue.types.Subscription.id
documentation: The subscriber UUID. This will be set by the client.

class_variables.name: gotrue.types.Subscription.callback
documentation: The function to call every time there is an event.

class_variables.name: gotrue.types.Subscription.unsubscribe
documentation: Call this to remove the listener.

classes.name: gotrue.types.SignInWithIdTokenCredentials
documentation: Provider name or OIDC `iss` value identifying which provider should be used to verify the provided token. Supported names: `google`, `apple`, `azure`, `facebook`, `kakao`, `keycloak` (deprecated).

class_variables.name: gotrue.types.MFAUnenrollParams.factor_id
documentation: ID of the factor being unenrolled.

class_variables.name: gotrue.types.CodeExchangeParams.code_verifier
documentation: Randomly generated string

class_variables.name: gotrue.types.CodeExchangeParams.auth_code
documentation: Code returned after completing one of the authorization flows

class_variables.name: gotrue.types.CodeExchangeParams.redirect_to
documentation: The URL to route to after a session is successfully obtained

class_variables.name: gotrue.types.MFAVerifyParams.factor_id
documentation: ID of the factor being verified.

class_variables.name: gotrue.types.MFAVerifyParams.challenge_id
documentation: ID of the challenge being verified.

class_variables.name: gotrue.types.MFAVerifyParams.code
documentation: Verification code provided by the user.

class_variables.name: gotrue.types.MFAChallengeParams.factor_id
documentation: ID of the factor to be challenged.

class_variables.name: gotrue.types.MFAChallengeAndVerifyParams.factor_id
documentation: ID of the factor being verified.

class_variables.name: gotrue.types.MFAChallengeAndVerifyParams.code
documentation: Verification code provided by the user.

class_variables.name: gotrue.types.AuthMFAVerifyResponse.access_token
documentation: New access token (JWT) after successful verification.

class_variables.name: gotrue.types.AuthMFAVerifyResponse.token_type
documentation: Type of token, typically `Bearer`.

class_variables.name: gotrue.types.AuthMFAVerifyResponse.expires_in
documentation: Number of seconds in which the access token will expire.

class_variables.name: gotrue.types.AuthMFAVerifyResponse.refresh_token
documentation: Refresh token you can use to obtain new access tokens when expired.

class_variables.name: gotrue.types.AuthMFAVerifyResponse.user
documentation: Updated user profile.

class_variables.name: gotrue.types.AuthMFAEnrollResponseTotp.qr_code
documentation:
Contains a QR code encoding the authenticator URI. You can
convert it to a URL by prepending `data:image/svg+xml;utf-8,` to
the value. Avoid logging this value to the console.

class_variables.name: gotrue.types.AuthMFAEnrollResponseTotp.secret
documentation:
The TOTP secret (also encoded in the QR code). Show this secret
in a password-style field to the user, in case they are unable to
scan the QR code. Avoid logging this value to the console.

class_variables.name: gotrue.types.AuthMFAEnrollResponseTotp.uri
documentation:
The authenticator URI encoded within the QR code, should you need
to use it. Avoid loggin this value to the console.

class_variables.name: gotrue.types.AuthMFAEnrollResponse.id
documentation: ID of the factor that was just enrolled (in an unverified state).

class_variables.name: gotrue.types.AuthMFAEnrollResponse.type
documentation: Type of MFA factor. Only `totp` supported for now.

class_variables.name: gotrue.types.AuthMFAEnrollResponse.totp
documentation: TOTP enrollment information.

class_variables.name: gotrue.types.AuthMFAEnrollResponse.friendly_name
documentation: Friendly name of the factor, useful for distinguishing between factors

class_variables.name: gotrue.types.AuthMFAEnrollResponse.phone
documentation: Phone number of the MFA factor in E.164 format. Used to send messages

class_variables.name: gotrue.types.AuthMFAUnenrollResponse.id
documentation: ID of the factor that was successfully unenrolled.

class_variables.name: gotrue.types.AuthMFAChallengeResponse.id
documentation: ID of the newly created challenge.

class_variables.name: gotrue.types.AuthMFAChallengeResponse.expires_at
documentation: Timestamp in UNIX seconds when this challenge will no longer be usable.

class_variables.name: gotrue.types.AuthMFAChallengeResponse.factor_type
documentation: Factor Type which generated the challenge

class_variables.name: gotrue.types.AuthMFAListFactorsResponse.all
documentation: All available factors (verified and unverified).

class_variables.name: gotrue.types.AuthMFAListFactorsResponse.totp
documentation: Only verified TOTP factors. (A subset of `all`.)

class_variables.name: gotrue.types.AuthMFAListFactorsResponse.phone
documentation: Only verified Phone factors. (A subset of `all`.)

class_variables.name: gotrue.types.AuthMFAGetAuthenticatorAssuranceLevelResponse.current_level
documentation: Current AAL level of the session.

class_variables.name: gotrue.types.AuthMFAGetAuthenticatorAssuranceLevelResponse.next_level
documentation:
Next possible AAL level for the session. If the next level is higher
than the current one, the user should go through MFA.

class_variables.name: gotrue.types.AuthMFAGetAuthenticatorAssuranceLevelResponse.current_authentication_methods
documentation:
A list of all authentication methods attached to this session. Use
the information here to detect the last time a user verified a
factor, for example if implementing a step-up scenario.

class_variables.name: gotrue.types.AuthMFAAdminDeleteFactorResponse.id
documentation: ID of the factor that was successfully deleted.

class_variables.name: gotrue.types.AuthMFAAdminDeleteFactorParams.id
documentation: ID of the MFA factor to delete.

class_variables.name: gotrue.types.AuthMFAAdminDeleteFactorParams.user_id
documentation: ID of the user whose factor is being deleted.

class_variables.name: gotrue.types.AuthMFAAdminListFactorsResponse.factors
documentation: All factors attached to the user.

class_variables.name: gotrue.types.AuthMFAAdminListFactorsParams.user_id
documentation: ID of the user for which to list all MFA factors.

classes.name: gotrue.types.GenerateLinkProperties

class_variables.name: gotrue.types.GenerateLinkProperties.action_link
documentation:
The email link to send to the user. The action_link follows the following format:

auth/v1/verify?type={verification_type}&token={hashed_token}&redirect_to={redirect_to}

class_variables.name: gotrue.types.GenerateLinkProperties.email_otp
documentation:
The raw email OTP.
You should send this in the email if you want your users to verify using an
OTP instead of the action link.

class_variables.name: gotrue.types.GenerateLinkProperties.hashed_token
documentation: The hashed token appended to the action link.

class_variables.name: gotrue.types.GenerateLinkProperties.redirect_to
documentation: The URL appended to the action link.

class_variables.name: gotrue.types.GenerateLinkProperties.verification_type
documentation: The verification type that the email link is associated to.
The properties related to the email link generated.

classes.name: gotrue._async.gotrue_admin_mfa_api.AsyncGoTrueAdminMFAAPI

methods.name: gotrue._async.gotrue_admin_mfa_api.AsyncGoTrueAdminMFAAPI.list_factors
documentation: Lists all factors attached to a user.

methods.name: gotrue._async.gotrue_admin_mfa_api.AsyncGoTrueAdminMFAAPI.delete_factor
documentation:
Deletes a factor on a user. This will log the user out of all active
sessions (if the deleted factor was verified). There's no need to delete
unverified factors.
Contains the full multi-factor authentication administration API.
